other_observers: As many as 60 others over four days.; Brandon Percival found it while we were leading a field trip for DFO.
description: Pink-legged thrush with strongly rufous tail, back, and richest on the crown of head. Underparts white with bold, somewhat arrowhead-shaped dark spots on breast and flanks. Bold white eyering, bill bicolored, pale yellowish at base and dark gray distally. Face grisled with tightly marked, dark grayish streaks.Behaviors: Spent most of the time on or very near the ground foraging in the litter and damp soil. When flushed, flew low and only a short distance. In same are on three days that I saw it.Call: nothing heard by mePlumage: alternate
